questions.question: Which interface-level command configures a router as a DHCP relay agent?
questions.explanation: 'ip helper-address <dhcp-server-ip>' configured on the client-facing interface makes the router forward DHCP broadcasts as unicast to the central DHCP server.

questions.question: On which interface should 'ip helper-address' be configured?
options: The interface facing the DHCP server
The interface facing the DHCP clients (the subnet with hosts needing addresses)
questions.explanation: The helper address must be configured on the client-facing interface — the one that receives the broadcast DHCP Discover.

questions.question: What does a DHCP relay agent do with a DHCP Discover message from a client?
options: Drops it because DHCP broadcasts cannot cross subnets
Converts the broadcast into a unicast aimed at the configured DHCP server
Replies to the client with its own IP as the DHCP server
Encrypts it and forwards it to the ISP
questions.explanation: A DHCP relay agent performs broadcast-to-unicast conversion, forwarding the DHCP message as unicast to the central DHCP server.

questions.question: When is a DHCP relay agent needed?
options: When a DHCP server lives in the same broadcast domain as the clients
When the router is itself a DHCP server
When DHCP clients are in the router's LAN but no DHCP server is in that LAN
When clients use static IP addresses
questions.explanation: A relay is needed when clients broadcast DHCP on a LAN that has no DHCP server — the router forwards those broadcasts to a DHCP server on another subnet.

questions.question: Which command configures a router interface to obtain its IP address from DHCP?
questions.explanation: 'ip address dhcp' under interface config makes the router act as a DHCP client and learn an IP for that interface.

questions.question: Does 'ip helper-address' forward only DHCP traffic?
options: No — it forwards several broadcast-based services by default, not just DHCP
questions.explanation: 'ip helper-address' forwards several broadcast-based UDP services (TFTP, DNS, NTP, NetBIOS, etc.) by default, in addition to DHCP.

questions.question: Which command verifies whether an interface received its IP via DHCP and shows any helper address configured?
questions.explanation: 'show ip interface <if>' shows helper addresses and indicates whether the IP was learned via DHCP.

questions.question: After a relay agent forwards a DHCP Discover to a central DHCP server, what source IP does the server see?
questions.explanation: The relay rewrites the source address to its own interface IP so the DHCP server can tell which subnet to pull a pool from.

questions.question: Which statement about a centralized DHCP server model is correct?
options: A single server can service many subnets, but relay agents are required on each client LAN
Each subnet must run its own DHCP server
The DHCP server must be in the same broadcast domain as every client
Clients must be configured with static DNS
questions.explanation: Centralized DHCP means one server handles multiple subnets — which requires relay agents on the client-side routers so broadcasts reach the remote server.

questions.question: Which command on the DHCP server (not the relay) shows current leases and client MAC addresses?
questions.explanation: 'show ip dhcp binding' on the DHCP server displays active leases along with client IP and MAC.
